rulings.Case Title: LTC (BVI) LIMITED, A BRITISH VIRGIN ISLANDS VS. BRAUNHAGEY & BORDEN LLP, A LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF LTC (BVI) LIMITED, A BRITISH VIRGIN ISLANDS LIMITED COMPANY'S MOTION IN SUPPORT OF LTC (BVI) LIMITED'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF DISCOVERY REFEREE; TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO ITS REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; AND FOR SANCTIONS. (ADDED PER ORDER FILED ON 1/6/25)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2025, line 2, PLAINTIFF LTC (BVI) LIMITED, A BRITISH VIRGIN ISLANDS LIMITED COMPANY'S MOTION IN SUPPORT OF LTC (BVI) LIMITED'S MOTION FOR APPOINTMENT OF DISCOVERY REFEREE; TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO ITS REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; AND FOR SANCTIONS. (ADDED PER ORDER FILED ON 1/6/25) The parties' joint filing indicates they have agreed to the appointment of Hon. Wynne S. Carvill (Ret.) (JAMS, 2 Embarcadero Center, Suite 1500, San Francisco, CA 94111, (415) 982-5267, SBN 76019) as the discovery referee in this case. The court makes this appointment. The court incorporates by reference its prior order of January 6, 2025 concerning the matters specified in Rule of Court 3.922, subdivisions (c) through (f). The parties do not indicate the extent court facilities and personnel may need to be used and so the court presumes that none are sought. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CV)

rulings.Case Title: KELLY ANN MCCLEARY VS. PACIFIC NEUROPHYSIOLOGICAL SYSTEMS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant?S Supplemental Form Interrogatories ? General, Set One, Supplemental Form Interrogatories ? Employment, Set One, Supplemental Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2025, line 1 DEFENDANT PACIFIC NEUROPHYSIOLOGICAL SYSTEMS LLC Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant?S Supplemental Form Interrogatories General, Set One, Supplemental Form Interrogatories Employment, Set One, Supplemental Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Three Defendant Pacific Neurophysiological Systems LLC's motion to compel further responses is granted. The requested items are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence, and as to its requests for production, Defendant has stated specific facts showing why further production is warranted. Plaintiff Kelly Ann McCleary is ordered to provide substantive responses in compliance with the Code of Civil Procedure to Defendant's Supplemental Form Interrogatories - General, Set One; Supplemental Form Interrogatories - Employment, Set One; Supplemental Requests for Production of Documents, Sets One and Two; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Requests for Production of Documents, Set Three. Plaintiff is ordered to provide these responses within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. Defendant also seeks sanctions. Sanctions shall be entered except where the party who unsuccessfully opposes a discovery motion acted with substantial justification or other circumstances make the entry of sanctions unjust. Here, the court does not find that Plaintiff acted with substantial justification but notes that Plaintiff's counsel withdrew after the motion was filed and Plaintiff has not apparently obtained new counsel. The court also notes, based on Plaintiff's appearance at the hearing on her prior counsel's motion to withdraw as well as the notice of this discovery motion hearing which was given in the order on that motion, that Plaintiff had notice of this hearing and Defendant's motion. The court awards $250 in sanctions to Defendant, payable via Defendant's counsel. Plaintiff shall pay the sanctions within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. rulings.Case Title: PAKAL TECHNOLOGIES, INC. VS. ROBERT BOSCH SEMICONDUCTOR LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2025, line 2, 2 - DEFENDANT ROBERT BOSCH SEMICONDUCTOR LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Bosch moves to strike those portions of the FAC that demand special damages on the grounds that an entitlement to special damages has not been pleaded with sufficient particularity. "A defendant cannot be presumed to be aware of the special damage resulting from his act, and therefore, in order to prevent a surprise on him, this sort of damage must be specially set forth in the complaint, or the plaintiff will not be permitted to give evidence of it. And an allegation that by reason of the breaches of contract the party has suffered special damages in a named sum, is not enough. The facts as to special damages must be stated with particularity. The amount of such damages must be stated with particularity. The amount of such damages must be given, and the means of occasioning them must be set forth." (Shook v. Pearson (1950) 99 Cal.App.2d 348, 351-352.) Pakal has pleaded damage to its business greater than the loss of its ability to have its semiconductors manufactured alone; it has pleaded that it is a nascent business that has lost reputation and future prospects because of the timing of the alleged breach. But it has not stated the amount of damages and has not stated the "means of occasioning them" beyond broad assertions. Pakal's contrary case authority, MacLeod v. Tribune Publishing Company (1959) 52 Cal.2d 536, 548, acknowledges that a plaintiff may not know the full amount of the special damages it has suffered at the time of pleading but does not excuse pleading those amounts that are known or reasonably estimated. The motion to strike is granted. Pakal has requested leave to amend to cure the defect; that leave is granted in keeping with California's liberal pleading standards. Pakal may file an amended complaint within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. Bosch's time to respond is extended to 20 days after service of the amended complaint. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: BRIAN DE LEMOS VS. BISHOP FOX INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2025, line 5 DEFENDANT VINCENT LIU , AN INDIVIDUAL, BISHOP FOX INC., STACH & LIU, LLC DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* Defendants Bishop Fox Inc. (collectively Bishop Fox) et al demur to Plaintiff Brian de Lemos's First Amended Complaint (FAC). The court overrules the demurrer except as to Count 2 for defamation; the court sustains the demurrer as to this count with leave to amend. This court previously took the demurrer off calendar on December 20, 2024 because the opposing party de Lemos did not provide courtesy copies of his papers in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.7B. The court ordered de Lemos to comply. De Lemos has not provided the court with courtesy copies and the court therefore orders him to show cause why he should not be sanctioned in the amount of $250 for failure to comply with the court's December 20, 2024 order. The OSC is set for hearing on March 21, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 301. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: DESIRAE FERIA VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 6, 2025, line 7, 2 - DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, MICHELLE ZAPATA MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Defendants San Francisco Unified School District and Michelle Zapata's motion to strike portions of Plaintiff Desirae Feria's complaint is granted. Feria opposes only the request to strike punitive damages allegations against Zapata. These allegations, however, fall short of showing fraud, oppression, or malice. Feria has leave to amend her complaint to allege facts supporting a punitive damages request. Feria's first amended complaint must be filed and served by February 25, 2025.
